blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
Here are the best Android apps available on the Google Play Store for playing **Blind Man's Bluff** (also widely known as **Indian Poker** or forehead poker):

### 1. **Blindex - Blind Card Game**
* **Overview:** This is a highly polished, single-player offline card game built specifically around the rules of Blind Man's Bluff. 
* **How it works:** You play head-to-head against an AI opponent. You can see the AI's card, but your own card remains hidden from you. 
* **Key Features:** 
  * Features a strategic betting system with options like *Call*, *Half*, *Die* (Fold), and *All-In*.
  * Includes an "expression mind game" where you must read the AI's facial expressions to guess your card's value, and you can change your own avatar's expressions (like smirking or sweating) to trick the AI.
  * Three AI difficulty levels (Easy, Normal, Hard).
  * 100% offline play with no Wi-Fi required.

### 2. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **Overview:** A multiplayer-focused adaptation of the game designed to be played with friends and family.
* **How it works:** True to the classic party game, you must predict whether you will win based entirely on looking at your opponent's card. 
* **Key Features:** 
  * Clean, straightforward interface optimized for mobile play.
  * Focuses heavily on the psychological aspect of the game, encouraging players to read each other's tells and expressions.

### 3. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **Overview:** A simple, lightweight simulation app designed for group play.
* **How it works:** It acts as a digital deck of cards specifically set up for multiple people to play the forehead-poker style of Blind Man's Bluff together.
* **Key Features:** 
  * Minimalist design that gets straight to the gameplay.
  * Ideal if you have a group of friends together in the same room and want to use your phones instead of a physical deck of cards.

*(Note: When searching the Play Store, avoid standard "Teen Patti" apps. While Teen Patti is sometimes marketed as "Indian Poker," it is actually a three-card brag/poker variant and does not feature the "card-on-the-forehead" mechanic of Blind Man's Bluff).*

forehead poker app Android.response:
If you are looking for a **forehead poker** app on Android, the game is also widely known as **Indian Poker** or **Blind Man's Bluff**. In this game, you can see your opponents' cards but not your own, and you must bet based on their reactions and what you can see. 

Depending on whether you want to play a digital version of the card game or a party game where you hold the phone to your forehead, here are the best real apps available on the Google Play Store:

### 1. Blindex - Blind Card Game
* **What it is:** A dedicated single-player, offline card game built entirely around the rules of Blind Man's Bluff (Forehead Poker). 
* **How it works:** You play head-to-head against an AI dealer. Your card faces outward (visible to the AI), and the AI's card is visible to you, but you cannot see your own card. You have to read the AI's "expressions" and betting patterns to guess your card's value and decide whether to Call, Half, Die (Fold), or go All-In. It features three difficulty levels and requires no Wi-Fi.

### 2. Indian Poker - Card Game (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **What it is:** A multiplayer-focused digital version of the classic forehead poker game.
* **How it works:** Designed to be played with friends and family, this app lets you play the classic "look at your opponent's card only" game. It is a great option if you want to play the psychological card game locally with other people using your mobile devices.

### 3. Indian poker (by Kanbe)
* **What it is:** A straightforward, lightweight simulation app designed to help you play Indian poker.
* **How it works:** It serves as a digital deck/tool to facilitate playing the card-on-forehead game with multiple people in person without needing a physical deck of cards. 

---

### Alternative "Forehead" Party Games
If you meant "forehead games" in the style of charades (where you hold the phone to your forehead and guess words based on your friends' clues), these are the top Android options:

* **Heads Up! (by Ellen Digital Ventures):** The official, incredibly popular party game where you select a category (like movies, celebrities, or animals), place the phone on your forehead, and try to guess the words as your friends shout clues. You tilt the phone down for a correct answer and up to skip.
* **Charades: Heads Up Game:** A highly-rated free alternative to Heads Up! that features over 20 fun categories. You hold the phone to your forehead, and your friends act out, sing, or give clues to help you guess the word before the timer runs out.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
If you are looking for a card game where you cannot see your own cards, there are two main game concepts that fit this description: **Hanabi** (a cooperative game where you see everyone's cards except your own) and **Indian Poker / Blind Man's Poker** (a competitive party game where you hold your card to your forehead). 

The following real, existing Android apps on the Google Play Store feature these mechanics:

### 1. Boardible: Games for Groups
* **What it is:** A multiplayer board game platform app.
* **How it fits:** **Boardible** features an official digital version of **Hanabi**, the world-famous cooperative card game. In Hanabi, players work together to build a fireworks show by playing cards in numerical order. The twist is that you hold your cards facing outward—you can see everyone else's hand, but you have no idea what cards you are holding. You must rely on limited clues from your teammates to figure out what to play or discard.

### 2. Indian Poker - Card Game (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **What it is:** A digital adaptation of the classic party game "Indian Poker" (also known as Blind Man's Poker).
* **How it fits:** In this game, you are dealt a card that is hidden from you but visible to your opponents (mimicking the real-life action of sticking a playing card to your forehead). You must look at your opponents' cards, read their expressions, and use psychological bluffing to guess if your card is higher or lower than theirs before placing your bets.

### 3. Indian poker (by Kanbe)
* **What it is:** A lightweight, local multiplayer simulation of the forehead-card game.
* **How it fits:** This app is designed to let a group of friends play Indian Poker using a single Android device. It handles the card dealing and keeps your own card hidden from your view while displaying it to the rest of the players, making it a great tool for a quick party game.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The style of game you are looking for is commonly referred to as a "forehead guessing game" or "headbands charades." In these games, one player holds the phone to their forehead with the screen facing outward while other players act out, sing, or give clues to help them guess the word on the screen. 

The best and most popular Android apps for this on the Google Play Store include:

### 1. **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)
This is the original, incredibly popular game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres. 
* **How it works:** You select a deck (such as Celebrities, Movies, Animals, or Accents), hold the phone to your forehead, and try to guess the words based on your friends' clues before the 60-second timer runs out. 
* **Controls:** Tilt the phone down when you guess correctly, or tilt it up to skip. It also has a feature that uses your phone's front camera to record your friends' hilarious reactions while they give you clues.

### 2. **Heads Up! Netflix Edition**
If you have an active Netflix subscription, you can play this version of the game for free without ads or in-app purchases.
* **How it works:** It features the exact same "hold to forehead" gameplay mechanics as the original, but includes exclusive decks themed around popular Netflix shows and movies (like *Stranger Things*, *Squid Game*, and *Bridgerton*).

### 3. **Charades & Headbands Game** (also known as *Charades App - What am I?*)
A highly-rated, free alternative to the official Heads Up! app.
* **How it works:** It features over 60 themed decks (including anime, video games, TV shows, and music) and allows you to easily create your own custom decks. You hold the phone to your forehead and tilt up to mark a correct answer or tilt down to pass.

### 4. **Headbands: Charades Party Game**
Another excellent, user-friendly option designed for parties and family gatherings.
* **How it works:** It features more than 40 unique categories (such as "Act It Out," "WWE," "Sports," and "Cartoons"). It uses the classic tilt-down to score and tilt-up to skip controls, making it very easy for players of all ages to pick up and play.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
Here are some of the best real, existing single-player, offline bluffing card games (and closely related bluffing games) available on the Google Play Store for Android:

### 1. **Cheat! – Bluffing Card Game**
* **Overview:** This is a dedicated offline adaptation of the classic card game "Cheat" (also known as Bluff, BS, or I Doubt It). 
* **How it works:** You play against three CPU opponents in fast-paced, intense bluffing battles. You place cards face-down and declare their rank—but you are allowed to lie. If an AI calls your bluff and catches you, you take a penalty; if they are wrong, they take the cards.
* **Offline Features:** Fully playable offline with strategic items (like Lie Counters and Bluff Shields), a title system (Liar, Honest, Hunter), and daily offline missions.

### 2. **Bluff Card Game Offline** (by SNG Games)
* **Overview:** Another excellent, clean adaptation of the classic "I Doubt It" / "Cheat" card game designed specifically for offline play.
* **How it works:** The goal is to be the first to empty your hand. You play cards face-down and declare their rank, trying to trick the AI into accepting your lies. 
* **Offline Features:** Features a 2-player mode (1v1 against a smart AI) and a 4-player mode (full-table action against multiple CPUs). It requires absolutely no internet connection and features dynamic AI difficulty.

### 3. **Liar's Cards - Ship of Deceit** (by Prism113)
* **Overview:** A dark, pirate-themed bluffing card game heavily inspired by the popular "Liar's Bar" style of gameplay.
* **How it works:** Set on a cursed pirate ship, players take turns playing cards face-down and declaring their rank (like Kings or Jokers). If you suspect someone is lying, you call them out. If you are caught bluffing, you must play the "poisoned mug challenge"—a Russian roulette-style mini-game where you drink rum and hope it isn't poisoned.
* **Offline Features:** While it has online multiplayer, it features single-player modes where you can test your poker face against AI opponents.

### 4. **Governor of Poker 2 - OFFLINE** (by Youda Games Holding B.V.)
* **Overview:** If you want the ultimate card game of bluffing, Texas Hold'em Poker is the gold standard. This is a massive, story-driven single-player poker RPG that is fully playable offline.
* **How it works:** You travel through the Wild West, visiting different cities and saloons to play poker against over 80 AI cowboys, each with their own distinct playing and bluffing personalities. You must read their tells, bluff your way through bad hands, and win enough chips to buy up real estate and conquer Texas.
* **Offline Features:** No Wi-Fi or internet connection is required to play the entire campaign.

### 5. **Liar's Dice** (by YOAMB B.V.)
* **Overview:** While this game uses dice instead of cards, it is one of the most famous bluffing games in the world (also known as Perudo or Pirate's Dice). 
* **How it works:** Players roll dice under a cup, keep their results secret, and take turns bidding on how many dice of a certain face value are on the table. You must bluff about your own roll and call out other players when you think their bid is too high.
* **Offline Features:** Includes a robust "Offline Practice" mode where you can play against highly intelligent AI offline to sharpen your bluffing and probability skills.

best offline single player card game And.response:
Here are some of the best offline, single-player card games available on the Google Play Store for Android:

### 1. **Balatro**
* **Genre:** Poker-themed Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** *Balatro* is an incredibly addictive game where you play illegal poker hands to score points and defeat "blinds". You can buy game-changing Joker cards, tarot cards, and planet cards to break the rules, create massive score multipliers, and build insane combos. It is fully playable offline and perfect for quick or long sessions.

### 2. **Slay the Spire**
* **Genre:** Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** Widely considered the gold standard of the deckbuilding genre, *Slay the Spire* tasks you with climbing a procedurally generated spire. You choose one of four characters—each with their own unique card pool and mechanics—and slowly build a synergistic deck to defeat challenging bosses. It is a premium game with zero ads or microtransactions, and it runs flawlessly offline.

### 3. **Dawncaster: Deckbuilding RPG**
* **Genre:** RPG Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** Designed specifically for mobile, *Dawncaster* is played in portrait mode and features deep RPG mechanics. You choose a class (like Warrior, Rogue, or Seeker), equip weapons, and build a deck from over 1,000 handcrafted cards. It is a premium, offline-first game with high replayability, rich lore, and no pay-to-win mechanics.

### 4. **Night of the Full Moon**
* **Genre:** Dark Fantasy Roguelike Card Game
* **Why it’s great:** Based on a dark retelling of the Little Red Riding Hood fairy tale, this game has you venture into the Black Forest to find your missing grandmother. You turn "pages" of a book to trigger random events, fight monsters, and buy cards. It features multiple distinct classes, beautiful hand-drawn art, and is highly optimized for offline play.

### 5. **Card Thief**
* **Genre:** Stealth Puzzle Card Game
* **Why it’s great:** If you want something different from traditional deckbuilders, *Card Thief* is a solitaire-style game played on a 3x3 grid. You play as a thief sneaking through the shadows, using stealth points to avoid guards, extinguish torches, and steal treasure. It is a brilliant, tactical puzzle game that requires careful planning and works perfectly offline.

### 6. **Pirates Outlaws**
* **Genre:** Pirate Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** Similar in gameplay to *Slay the Spire*, *Pirates Outlaws* has you navigate dangerous seas, manage your "ammo" resource, and battle outlaw crews. It features a large roster of unlockable characters (each with unique starting decks and abilities) and multiple maps to explore. It is highly polished, challenging, and fully playable offline.

forehead poker app Android.response:

- **Pokerheads - Heads-up Poker** — A heads-up poker app focused on **1v1 play**, with AI tips and training features.[6]
- **HeadsUp Poker: 5-Hand Showdown** — More of a multiplayer poker variant, but still in the same “heads-up poker” family.[5]

If you want the app that most closely matches the *forehead / reveal-your-card* party-game feel, **Heads Up Poker** is the best fit.[10] If you want a more standard online poker app with friend play, **Poker Face: Texas Holdem Poker** is the stronger pick.[1][9]

best offline single player card game And.response:
If you want the **best offline single-player card games on Android**, the strongest general picks are **Solitaire**-style games, especially **Microsoft Solitaire Collection**, **Solitaire – Classic Card Games** by MobilityWare, and **Card Thief** for something more strategic. These are real, widely available Android apps that work well without an internet connection.

- **Microsoft Solitaire Collection** — the most polished all-around choice for classic solo card play, with multiple built-in modes like Klondike, Spider, FreeCell, Pyramid, and TriPeaks.
- **Solitaire – Classic Card Games** by MobilityWare — a clean, reliable offline solitaire app with a simple interface and strong single-player focus.
- **Card Thief** — a single-player card-stealth game rather than traditional solitaire; it is a good pick if you want more strategy and progression.
- **Solitaire Grand Harvest** — a more casual, level-based solitaire game with offline-friendly play for short sessions.
- **Aces® Spades** — good if you specifically want a classic trick-taking card game against AI opponents in single-player mode.

If you want the **best overall offline experience**, I’d start with **Microsoft Solitaire Collection** for classic card-game variety and **Card Thief** if you want the most interesting single-player design.
